data.description: Experiments with driverless cars have been underway for decades — but only now are they becoming a reality. So what does it actually feel like? CGTN’s Michael Marillier and Wang Tianyu took a ride to find out. Would you hop into a self-driving car?

data.title: Vermilion Energy Inc. declares $0.135 CDN cash dividend for March 31, 2026, payable to shareholders on record by March 13.
data.description: Calgary-based Vermilion Energy Inc. has disclosed a cash dividend of $0.135 CDN per common share. The dividend will be distributed on March 31, 2026, to shareholders recorded on March 13, 2026. Vermilion is listed on both the Toronto Stock Exchange (TSX: VET) and the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE: VET). This dividend is part of the company's quarterly payouts and demonstrates its commitment to providing returns to shareholders.

data.title: Nanalysis Corp grants 1,210,000 RSUs and 350,000 Options to directors and officers.
data.description: In Calgary, AB on March 4, 2026, Nanalysis Scientific Corp. disclosed that their Board of Directors approved the allocation of 1,210,000 restricted share units (RSUs) and 350,000 stock options (Options) to specific directors and officers. The Company, trading as NSCI on the TSXV and 1N1 on the FRA, made this decision regarding the mentioned equity awards.

data.title: Waste expert warns against improper battery disposal in South San Francisco; article reveals dangers.
data.description: In South San Francisco, on March 4, 2026, a recent article from HelloNation highlights the significant risk posed by household batteries when improperly disposed of in the trash. This issue is prevalent in South San Francisco and throughout San Mateo County. The article delves into the reasons behind this danger, shedding light on the potential consequences of commonly made mistakes in battery disposal.

data.title: Kinaxis Inc. (TSX:KXS) reports record Q4 2025 results in USD, Dec 31, 2025, reflecting increased demand volatility.
data.description: Global supply chain orchestration leader Kinaxis® (TSX:KXS) based in Ottawa, Ontario, unveiled remarkable numbers for the fourth quarter concluded on December 31, 2025. The financial figures are stated in U.S. dollars and are aligned with IFRS Accounting Standards. The company expressed satisfaction with achieving record-breaking results for both the fourth quarter and fiscal year 2025. This success underscores the rising necessity for businesses to navigate exceedingly high levels of demand fluctuations.

data.title: Kinaxis CFO, Blaine Fitzgerald, to step down; search for replacement underway; resignation effective post Q1 2026 earnings call.
data.description: Kinaxis® Inc. (TSX:KXS), a key player in supply chain orchestration, revealed that Blaine Fitzgerald, the current Chief Financial Officer (CFO), will be resigning to pursue an opportunity with a private firm unrelated to supply chain software. A search for a successor will commence promptly as Fitzgerald will continue as CFO until May 8, post the company's first quarter 2026 earnings call.

data.title: Actuate Therapeutics to present at Citizens Life Sciences Conference on March 10, 2026 in Miami Beach, FL at 8:25am ET. CEO, Dan Schmitt, to present.
data.description: On March 04, 2026, Actuate Therapeutics, Inc. (NASDAQ: ACTU), a biopharmaceutical company based in Chicago and Fort Worth, revealed that its President & CEO, Dan Schmitt, is set to speak at the Citizens Life Sciences Conference in Miami Beach, FL on March 10, 2026. Actuate focuses on developing treatments for challenging cancers by targeting glycogen synthase kinase-3 beta (GSK-3β). The presentation will occur at 8:25 a.m. ET.

data.title: Ring Energy (AMEX: REI) Q4 earnings missed estimates by -33.33%, with EPS at $0.02 vs. expected $0.03, revenue down $16.56M.
data.description: Ring Energy (AMEX: REI) released its Q4 earnings report on March 4, 2026, at 04:45 PM. The company fell short of expected earnings by -33.33%, with an EPS of $0.02 instead of the estimated $0.03. Revenue decreased by $16.56 million compared to the previous year. For more in-depth analysis of their earnings history, investors can access the complete story on Benzinga.com.

data.title: SCYNEXIS reports Q4 earnings; EPS $0.25 beats estimate by 31.58%. Revenue up $17.67 million YoY.
data.description: SCYNEXIS (NASDAQ: SCYX) disclosed its Q4 earnings on March 4, 2026, at 04:20 PM. The report revealed that the company exceeded expectations with an EPS of $0.25, surpassing the estimated $0.19 by 31.58%. Revenue also saw a notable increase of $17.67 million compared to the corresponding period last year. For further insights into SCYNEXIS's past earnings performance, refer to the detailed article on Benzinga.com.

data.title: U.S. stock futures slip after GDP report reveals 1.4% growth in Q4 2025, missing expectations. Inflation rises to 2.9% in December. Dow Jones -0.21%, S&P 500 -0.27%, Nasdaq 100 -0.37%, Russell 2000 -0.57%. Dropbox down 4.16% despite better-than-expected earnings for Q4. President Trump warns Iran, Treasury bond yield at 4.07%. Fed likely to keep interest rates steady in March with 94% likelihood.
data.description: U.S. stock futures declined after economic data showed a sharp slowdown in the economy, with GDP growing at a 1.4% rate in the fourth quarter, below expectations. The PCE price index rose to 2.9% year over year in December. President Trump warned Iran, leading to tensions. The 10-year Treasury bond yielded 4.07% and the two-year bond was at 3.47%. Markets priced a 94% likelihood of the Fed keeping interest rates unchanged in March. Stock index performance showed declines across the board, with the Dow Jones down 0.21%, S&P 500 down 0.27%, Nasdaq 100 down 0.37%, and Russell 2000 down 0.57%. The SPY and QQQ ETFs, tracking S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 respectively, also dipped in premarket trading. Dropbox Inc. saw a 4.16% drop despite beating Q4 earnings, with a sales forecast of $2.485-2.5 billion for FY2026. Full story at Benzinga.com.

data.title: Kevin Warsh's shift from inflation hawk to Trump ally has left Fed watchers puzzled about the future direction of the central bank.
data.description: Observers of the Federal Reserve have expressed confusion over Kevin Warsh's transformation from a staunch inflation hawk during the crisis to an ally of President Trump. Many are now questioning which iteration of Warsh will ultimately guide the central bank's policies.

data.title: Nasdaq rises after Supreme Court rules against Trump's global tariffs; Nvidia shares up. Live coverage by Investor's Business Daily.
data.description: The stock market saw a turnaround today as stocks rose following the Supreme Court's decision to reject President Trump's international tariffs. The Nasdaq index surged upwards, with a notable increase in Nvidia shares. This news was covered live by Investor's Business Daily.

data.title: SCOTUS invalidates Trump's tariffs. He imposed import taxes on goods from 100+ nations using the 1977 IEEPA.
data.description: The International Emergency Economic Powers Act of 1977 was utilized by President Trump to implement tariffs on imported goods from over 100 nations, making him the initial president to do so.

data.title: Five Democratic gubernatorial candidates endorse San Francisco's Overpaid CEO Act, backing fair share payment for large corps to protect vital programs.
data.description: As of March 4, 2026, candidates have united with an expanding coalition in San Francisco, demanding that large corporations operating in the city contribute their fair share towards safeguarding essential programs and services. This initiative aims to prevent severe cutbacks that could negatively impact the residents of San Francisco.

Lantern is one of the most iconic symbols of the Lantern Festival in China, representing the warding off of misfortune and prayers for peace and reunion. Like a cluster of glowing sparks, this song lights up the night with hope and wishes for happiness.

data.title: Omega Flex, Inc. CEO announces 2025 net sales at $98.3M, down 3.3% from 2024. Q4 sales dropped 6.5%.companyId=22002
data.description: Dean W. Rivest, CEO of Omega Flex, Inc., recently disclosed that the company's net sales in 2025 amounted to $98,296,000, dropping from $101,681,000 in 2024, marking a decrease of $3,385,000 or 3.3%. The net sales for the fourth quarter of 2025 were 6.5% lower than the same period in 2024. This decline was primarily driven by reduced sales unit volumes, influenced by factors such as a downturn in housing starts leading to overall market suppression.
